title: The Selection Framework
concept: Selecting an LLM depends on tradeoffs: accuracy, latency, hardware hosting cost, customization requirements, and data privacy.

exercises.prompt: When building an app with AI, what is the most important thing to do-it-yourself main goal of your main goal should be to...

options.text: Balance trade-offs like speed and cost

exercises.explain: Choosing an AI model requires balancing different priorities, just like deciding between a fast sports car, a cheap bike, or a heavy truck.

cards.title: The LLM Shopping List
cards.body: With thousands of AI models out there, how do you pick the perfect one for your app? It's not just about finding the 'smartest' model—it's about balancing speed, cost, and privacy.

cards.title: What is the Selection Framework?
cards.body: The Selection Framework is a guide to choosing the right AI model by balancing five key tradeoffs: Accuracy (how smart), Latency (how fast), Cost (how expensive to run), Customization (can you train it further), and Privacy (where the data lives).

cards.title: The Vehicle Analogy
cards.body: Choosing an LLM is like picking a vehicle. A heavy delivery truck (massive LLM) can carry huge loads (complex reasoning) but is slow and burns a lot of fuel. A bicycle (small, local LLM) is fast and free to run but can't carry heavy cargo.

cards.title: Smart Tradeoffs in Action
cards.body: Let's say you want to build a simple spelling corrector. Instead of renting a giant, expensive cloud supercomputer model, you can use a small, lightweight model that runs directly on a user's phone for free and works instantly!

cards.title: Myth: Bigger is Always Better
cards.body: It is easy to think that a bigger model is always better. However, massive models are often slow, very expensive to run, and might send your private data to external servers when a tiny, local model could do the job just fine!

cards.title: Weighing Your Options
cards.body: Depending on your app's goals, you will lean toward one of two main paths.

right.text: Super fast, free to run, keeps data private, but less knowledgeable.

left.text: Super smart, but slower, expensive, and sends data over the internet.

exercises.prompt: What is the best way to choose an LLM for your project?

options.text: Only pick the model with the absolute highest accuracy score.
Balance accuracy, speed, cost, customization, and privacy.
Always choose the cheapest model regardless of its quality.
Ignore privacy because all AI models are automatically secure.

exercises.explain: While accuracy is important, choosing an AI model requires balancing speed (latency), costs, security (privacy), and customization.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Hosting your own LLM locally gives you great data privacy, but you have to pay for the hardware to run it.
exercises.explain: Hosting an LLM on your own computers keeps data secure, but you must pay for the hardware power. Cloud models might be cheaper upfront but send data elsewhere.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the definition of speed in AI systems.
exercises.sentence: The time it takes for an LLM to respond to your prompt is called its latency.
exercises.explain: Latency refers to the delay before a transfer of data begins following an instruction. In AI, lower latency means faster replies!

exercises.prompt: Which developer is using the Selection Framework correctly?

options.text: Using a small, fast model to translate single words instantly on a smartwatch.
Using a giant supercomputer model to check if a user typed 'yes' or 'no'.
Sending private bank account numbers to a public search engine AI.

exercises.explain: Option A shows a smart tradeoff choice: using a fast, cheap model for a simple task, rather than wasting power and money.

exercises.prompt: Imagine you are building a medical app that needs fast responses, absolute data privacy (no sending patient files to third parties), and a low budget. What is your best strategy?

options.text: Send all data to a massive public cloud model over the internet
Host a smaller, customized model on local secure hardware
Use the biggest model available without checking costs
Avoid using AI entirely because of internet speed limits

exercises.explain: To protect sensitive medical data while minimizing internet delays and keeping costs predictable, hosting a smaller, customized model on your own local server is the best strategy.
